_comment: The CLIP classifier (stillnorth/classify.py, mirrored from tsn_classify.py) sorts each FLUX image by its CONTENT into A/B/C/D. Each content class is then animated with the Wan 2.2 motion prompt that physically fits that content. Edit the 'motion' strings freely -- they are sent verbatim to the Wan positive-prompt node. IMPORTANT (2026-07-09): the sampler runs at cfg=1 (4-step Seko distill) so the NEGATIVE prompt is ignored -- the positive prompt is the ONLY steering. Never ask for volumetrics in motion (drifting fog, falling snow, rolling clouds): Wan renders those as soft blurry blobs that spawn/dissolve mid-clip and compound over the continuation. Describe camera glide + stable weather + sharp detail instead.
_comment_falls: 2026-07-12 frozen-waterfall fix, two parts. (1) flux_suffixes: sources whose FLUX prompt mentions falling water get fast-shutter language appended -- FLUX's default long-exposure silky falls give Wan nothing to move (matched-seed A/B: silky falls stay frozen at ~0 px/frame regardless of motion prompt; a fast-shutter droplet-textured source unlocked +0.47 px/frame sustained downward flow). (2) classes.C.overrides: the same sources get a falls-specific motion prompt instead of the calm-water one, which at cfg=1 actively freezes falls.

flux_suffixes.suffix: Fast shutter speed: every waterfall is frozen in crisp detail, individual water droplets, streaks and white spray clearly visible and sharp -- absolutely no long-exposure silky smooth water effect.

_comment_camera: 2026-07-12 scene-aware camera. Poses are picked by WEIGHTED group, not uniform random: ~50% {up, dolly-back} vs 50% {dolly-forward, left, right}. On WanCameraEmbedding, Zoom In = T[0,0,+2] (dolly FORWARD into the scene) and Zoom Out = T[0,0,-2] (dolly BACK) -- true translations, not focal zoom -- so the existing five poses already span every translation axis; no new pose or workflow change is needed. Left/right keep matched speed (0.4). pose_block_map maps the image judge's Line-3 MOVE-BLOCK directions onto the poses they forbid, so the camera never dollies/pans straight into a near cliff wall or tree mass.

A.content: aerial forest canopy / snowy plains (land only, no open water)
A.motion: Smooth professional aerial drone footage gliding slowly over a vast forest canopy with gentle parallax. The scene stays exactly as it is: crisp, sharp, photorealistic, stable natural lighting, constant weather. Treetops sway almost imperceptibly. Any existing thin mist stays in place, calm and nearly still -- no new fog, no smoke, no haze thickening, nothing appears or disappears. Fine detail of branches, moss and terrain stays sharp and steady through the whole shot. High detail 4K nature documentary quality.

B.content: mountain ridges, peaks, drifting clouds and mist
B.motion: Smooth professional aerial drone footage gliding slowly along a mountain ridge with gentle parallax. Crisp, sharp, photorealistic, stable natural lighting, constant weather. The existing clouds and valley mist drift very slowly and keep their overall shape and coverage -- no new fog forming, no haze thickening, terrain never gets swallowed. Rock faces, snow patches and ridgelines keep their fine sharp detail steady through the whole shot. High detail 4K nature documentary quality.

C.content: rivers, lakes and open liquid water with reflections
C.motion: Smooth professional aerial drone footage gliding slowly over calm water with gentle parallax. Crisp, sharp, photorealistic, stable natural lighting, constant weather. The water moves gently -- a steady current with subtle surface ripples and soft reflections -- while the banks and treeline stay sharp and steady. No new mist forming, no haze thickening, nothing appears or disappears. High detail 4K nature documentary quality.

overrides.motion: Smooth professional aerial drone footage gliding slowly with gentle parallax over cliffs with waterfalls. Every waterfall pours continuously downward at natural speed -- water visibly streams and falls down the rock face in one steady direction the whole time, white water churning steadily where it lands. The rocks, cliffs and vegetation stay crisp, sharp and steady; no new mist forming, no haze thickening, nothing appears or disappears, constant weather and lighting. High detail 4K nature documentary quality.

D.content: glaciers, frozen lakes, arctic ice and snow-covered ground
D.motion: Smooth professional aerial drone footage gliding slowly over a frozen arctic landscape with gentle parallax. Crisp, sharp, photorealistic, stable natural lighting, constant clear weather. Ice, snow crust and meltwater channels glisten subtly as the viewpoint moves; every crack and texture in the ice keeps its fine sharp detail steady through the whole shot. No falling snow, no fog, no haze, nothing appears or disappears. High detail 4K nature documentary quality.
